topic.name: Coral Reef Conservation Legislation
topic.description: Legislative measures aimed at the conservation and restoration of coral reef ecosystems in the United States.

Document 1: Amends the Coral Reef Conservation Act of 2000 to transfer certain authority from the Administrator of the National Oceanic and Atmospheric Administration (NOAA) to the Secretary of Commerce.

Document 2: Amends the Coral Reef Conservation Act of 2000 to extend the award of remaining coral reef conservation program grant funds to appropriate projects.

Document 3: This bill amends the Coral Reef Conservation Act to authorize the 12 federal agencies on the U.S. Coral Reef Task Force to carry out, either individually or cooperatively, prize competitions that promote coral reef research and conservation.

Document 4: This bill amends the Coral Reef Conservation Act of 2000 to expand the coral reef conservation program by awarding remaining program grant funds to appropriate projects.

Document 5: Amends the Coral Reef Conservation Act of 2000 to direct the Secretary of Commerce to make grants for projects for the conservation of coral reef ecosystems.
Authorizes the Secretary to conduct activities, including with local, regional, or international programs and partners, to conserve coral reef ecosystems.
Document 6: This bill amends the Coral Reef Conservation Act of 2000 to extend the award of coral reef conservation program grant funds to appropriate projects.
NOAA must also submit an international coral reef ecosystem strategy to Congress and may establish an international coral reef ecosystem partnership program.
Document 7: Amends the Coral Reef Conservation Act of 2000 to direct the Secretary of Commerce to submit to specified congressional committees a national coral reef ecosystem action strategy.

Document 8: Amends the Coral Reef Conservation Act of 2000 to transfer certain authority from the Administrator of the National Oceanic and Atmospheric Administration (NOAA) to the Secretary of Commerce.

Document 9: Amends the Coral Reef Conservation Act of 2000 to extend the award of remaining coral reef conservation program grant funds to other appropriate projects.

Document 10: Amends the Coral Reef Conservation Act of 2000 to extend the award of remaining coral reef conservation program grant funds to appropriate projects.

Document 1: Authorizes the Altamont Corridor Rail Project for final design and construction of a new fixed guideway.
Allows for the use of any alternative analysis or environmental impact report relating the Project.
Document 2: Amends federal transportation law to authorize the Secretary of Transportation to designate high-speed rail infrastructure bonds.
Requires any entity providing high-speed intercity passenger railroad transportation to negotiate over hiring and working conditions.
Document 3: Establishes the Emergency Transportation Safety Fund.
Directs the Secretary of Transportation to compile a prioritized list of emergency transportation projects.
Document 4: Allows the local matching share provided by a public transportation capital project assistance recipient to include expenditures by a private commuter vanpooling service provider.
Authorizes such a private provider to use vanpool revenues exceeding operating costs to acquire such vans.
Document 5: Requires a rail carrier to have a storage plan for any of its rail cars that it stores for three or more years.
Continues to store on tracks that pass through a commercial- or residential-zoned area.
Document 6: Requires the Secretaries of various federal agencies to determine if any federal programs are duplicative.
Submit a report on the results and terminate or consolidate programs performing similar functions.
Document 7: Amends federal transportation law to authorize appropriations for railroad safety, including the safe transportation of hazardous materials.
Directs the Secretary to develop a long-term railroad safety strategy.
Document 8: Reauthorizes appropriations to the Secretary of Transportation for capital grants to states for rail line relocation and improvement projects.
Document 9: Revises requirements with respect to the consolidation, merger, or acquisition of control involving rail carriers.
Prohibits the Board from approving mergers if their adverse impacts on safety outweigh their transportation benefits.
Document 10: Directs the Under Secretary for Border and Transportation Security to complete a vulnerability assessment of freight and passenger rail transportation.
Sets forth certain whistleblower protections for rail employees or other persons who have provided information regarding public transportation security.

topic.name: Nuclear Energy and STEM Initiatives
topic.description: This cluster focuses on legislative acts that promote research, safety, and education in nuclear energy and STEM fields.

Document 1: Amends the Energy Policy Act of 2005 to direct the Secretary of Energy (DOE) to conduct research to lower the cost of nuclear reactor systems while increasing their levels of safety and security.
Includes among research subjects: modular and small-scale reactors, balance-of-plant issues, cost-efficient manufacturing and construction.
Document 2: Amends the Atomic Energy Act of 1954 to prohibit the Nuclear Regulatory Commission (NRC) from approving the request of a licensee for a waiver of, or exemption from, a covered regulation applicable to a civilian nuclear power reactor that has permanently ceased to operate.
Specifies as a covered regulation: an emergency preparedness or response regulation or requirement, or a security regulation or requirement applicable to spent nuclear fuel.
Document 3: Directs the Secretary of Education to establish and maintain a database of information on public and private programs of financial assistance for the study of postsecondary and graduate science, technology, engineering, and mathematics.
Requires that such database provide separate information for each field of study and be searchable by category and combinations of categories.
Document 4: Renames the Department of Energy High-End Computing Revitalization Act of 2004 as the Exascale Computing for Science, Competitiveness, Advanced Manufacturing, Leadership, and the Economy Act of 2013.
Directs the Secretary of Energy (DOE) to conduct a research program to develop exascale computing machines to promote DOE missions.
Document 5: Authorizes appropriations for FY2008-FY2012 to the National Science Foundation (NSF) for the Experimental Program to Stimulate Competitive Research (EPSCoR).
Requires the Director of the NSF to authorize states participating in the grant program to include partnership with out-of-state research institutions.
